weeks.title: Foundations: Complexity & Linear Structures
weeks.goal: Understand fundamental concepts of data structures and algorithms, including time/space complexity analysis, and master basic linear data structures like arrays, linked lists, stacks, and queues.

topics.name: Introduction to DSA & Algorithm Analysis
topics.description: Overview of Data Structures and Algorithms, their importance, and an introduction to analyzing algorithm efficiency using Big O notation (time and space complexity).

topics.name: Arrays and Basic Operations
topics.description: Understanding static and dynamic arrays, their memory layout, and common operations like insertion, deletion, searching, and traversal.

topics.name: Linked Lists (Singly, Doubly, Circular)
topics.description: Deep dive into linked lists, covering singly, doubly, and circular linked lists. Focus on node structure, traversal, insertion, and deletion operations.

topics.name: Stacks (LIFO)
topics.description: Understanding the Stack data structure, its LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) principle, and common operations (push, pop, peek, isEmpty). Implementation using arrays and linked lists.

topics.name: Queues (FIFO)
topics.description: Understanding the Queue data structure, its FIFO (First-In, First-Out) principle, and common operations (enqueue, dequeue, peek, isEmpty). Implementation using arrays and linked lists, including circular queues.

topics.name: Recursion Basics
topics.description: Introduction to recursive thinking, understanding base cases and recursive steps, and analyzing the call stack. Solving simple recursive problems.

weeks.title: Non-Linear Structures: Trees and Graphs
weeks.goal: Gain proficiency in non-linear data structures, specifically trees and graphs, including their various types, traversal methods, and basic applications.

topics.name: Binary Trees and Binary Search Trees (BSTs)
topics.description: Understanding general tree concepts, binary trees, and the properties of Binary Search Trees. Operations like insertion, deletion, and searching in BSTs.

topics.name: Tree Traversal (BFS, DFS, In/Pre/Post-order)
topics.description: Exploring different ways to traverse trees: Breadth-First Search (Level-Order) and Depth-First Search (Inorder, Preorder, Postorder) traversals.

topics.name: Heaps (Min/Max Heap) and Heap Sort
topics.description: Understanding heap properties, implementing min-heaps and max-heaps, and learning the Heap Sort algorithm for efficient sorting.

topics.name: Hash Tables and Hashing
topics.description: Introduction to hash tables, hash functions, collision resolution techniques (chaining, open addressing), and their applications for efficient data retrieval.

topics.name: Graphs: Representation
topics.description: Introduction to graph theory, types of graphs, and different ways to represent graphs (adjacency matrix, adjacency list).

topics.name: Graph Traversal (BFS, DFS)
topics.description: Implementing Breadth-First Search (BFS) and Depth-First Search (DFS) for graphs and understanding their applications.

weeks.title: Sorting, Searching, and Algorithmic Paradigms
weeks.goal: Master efficient sorting and searching algorithms, and begin exploring fundamental algorithmic design paradigms like greedy, divide and conquer, and dynamic programming.

topics.name: Sorting Algorithms: Merge Sort
topics.description: Understanding the Divide and Conquer paradigm through Merge Sort, its implementation, and complexity analysis.

topics.name: Sorting Algorithms: Quick Sort
topics.description: Understanding the Divide and Conquer paradigm through Quick Sort, its implementation, pivot selection strategies, and complexity analysis.

topics.name: Searching Algorithms: Binary Search (Revisited)
topics.description: In-depth look at Binary Search on sorted arrays, its recursive and iterative implementations, and applications.

topics.name: Greedy Algorithms
topics.description: Introduction to the Greedy paradigm, understanding its principles, and solving classic problems like Activity Selection and Fractional Knapsack.

topics.name: Divide and Conquer Paradigm
topics.description: Formalizing the Divide and Conquer strategy, analyzing its characteristics, and reviewing examples beyond sorting (e.g., Tower of Hanoi).

topics.name: Dynamic Programming (Introduction)
topics.description: Introduction to Dynamic Programming, understanding overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure. Solving basic DP problems using memoization and tabulation (e.g., Fibonacci, Coin Change).

weeks.title: Advanced Graph Algorithms & Problem Solving
weeks.goal: Explore advanced graph algorithms, apply various algorithmic paradigms to complex problems, and consolidate understanding for effective problem-solving.

topics.name: Minimum Spanning Trees (Prim's & Kruskal's)
topics.description: Understanding MSTs and implementing Prim's and Kruskal's algorithms for finding the minimum spanning tree in a weighted graph.

topics.name: Shortest Path Algorithms: Dijkstra's
topics.description: Implementing Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest path from a single source to all other vertices in a graph with non-negative edge weights.

topics.name: Backtracking
topics.description: Introduction to the Backtracking algorithmic paradigm, understanding how to explore all potential solutions systematically. Solving problems like N-Queens, Sudoku Solver, or Permutations.

topics.name: String Algorithms (Basic String Matching)
topics.description: Introduction to basic string manipulation and searching algorithms, such as naive pattern matching and an overview of more advanced techniques.

topics.name: Complexity Classes (P, NP, NP-Complete - Conceptual)
topics.description: A conceptual introduction to computational complexity theory, understanding P, NP, and NP-Complete problems without deep mathematical proofs.

topics.name: Review and Advanced Problem Solving Strategies
topics.description: Consolidating all learned data structures and algorithms, practicing mixed problems, and discussing strategies for approaching new algorithmic challenges.

weeks.title: Web Fundamentals & Front-End Basics
weeks.goal: Understand how the web works, build basic static web pages using HTML, style them with CSS, and grasp fundamental JavaScript concepts.

topics.name: Web Architecture, HTTP & DNS
topics.description: Explore the client-server model, how browsers communicate with servers, the role of HTTP/HTTPS protocols, and domain name resolution.

topics.name: HTML Structure & Semantics
topics.description: Learn to create well-structured web content using HTML5 elements, understand semantic tags, and build basic forms.

topics.name: CSS Basics & Box Model
topics.description: Style HTML elements using CSS selectors, properties, values, and understand the fundamental CSS box model for layout.

topics.name: CSS Layouts: Flexbox Introduction
topics.description: Dive into Flexbox for creating one-dimensional layouts and aligning items efficiently within containers.

topics.name: Introduction to JavaScript
topics.description: Learn JavaScript variables, data types, operators, and basic control flow (conditionals, loops) for dynamic behavior.

topics.name: Development Environment Setup
topics.description: Set up a robust development environment including a code editor (e.g., VS Code) and utilize browser developer tools for inspection and debugging.

weeks.title: Dynamic Front-End & Version Control
weeks.goal: Master responsive web design, make web pages interactive with JavaScript DOM manipulation and events, and begin using Git for version control.

topics.name: Advanced CSS: Grid & Responsive Design
topics.description: Explore CSS Grid for two-dimensional layouts and implement responsive design techniques using media queries for various screen sizes.

topics.name: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
topics.description: Interact with the Document Object Model (DOM) to select, create, modify, and remove HTML elements dynamically.

topics.name: JavaScript Event Handling
topics.description: Learn to capture and respond to user interactions (clicks, keypresses, form submissions) using event listeners.

topics.name: Asynchronous JavaScript & Fetch API
topics.description: Understand asynchronous operations using callbacks, Promises, and the Fetch API to make network requests without blocking the main thread.

topics.name: Forms & Client-side Validation
topics.description: Design user-friendly forms and implement client-side validation using HTML5 attributes and JavaScript to improve user experience.

topics.name: Version Control with Git & GitHub
topics.description: Introduction to Git for tracking code changes, creating branches, merging, and collaborating on projects using GitHub.

weeks.title: Back-End Development & Data Management
weeks.goal: Understand server-side programming, build basic APIs with Node.js and Express, and learn fundamental database concepts with both SQL and NoSQL.

topics.name: Introduction to Back-End Concepts
topics.description: Grasp the server-side architecture, the request-response lifecycle, and the role of server-side languages.

topics.name: Node.js & Express.js Fundamentals
topics.description: Set up a basic server using Node.js and the Express.js framework, define routes, and handle HTTP requests.

topics.name: RESTful API Design Principles
topics.description: Learn to design clean and scalable APIs following REST principles, utilizing HTTP methods for resource manipulation.

topics.name: Introduction to Databases (SQL)
topics.description: Understand relational database concepts, SQL queries (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE), and database schema design.

topics.name: Introduction to Databases (NoSQL)
topics.description: Explore NoSQL databases, specifically document-based databases like MongoDB, and perform basic CRUD operations.

topics.name: Connecting Front-End to Back-End
topics.description: Learn to fetch data from your back-end API using client-side JavaScript and display it on the front-end.

topics.name: Middleware in Express.js
topics.description: Understand and implement middleware functions in Express.js for tasks like logging, authentication, and error handling.

weeks.title: Security, Deployment & Advanced Topics
weeks.goal: Implement user authentication, understand web security best practices, learn deployment strategies, and explore real-time communication.

topics.name: User Authentication: Sessions & Cookies
topics.description: Implement user login and registration, managing user sessions using cookies and session IDs on the server-side.

topics.name: User Authorization: Access Control
topics.description: Learn to implement authorization mechanisms to control what authenticated users can access or modify based on their roles or permissions.

topics.name: Deployment Fundamentals
topics.description: Understand the basics of deploying web applications to hosting platforms, configuring domains, and setting up server environments.

topics.name: Web Security Best Practices
topics.description: Identify common web vulnerabilities like XSS, CSRF, and SQL Injection, and learn strategies to prevent them in your applications.

topics.name: Error Handling & Debugging
topics.description: Develop robust error handling strategies for both front-end and back-end, and master debugging techniques for efficient problem-solving.

topics.name: WebSockets for Real-time Applications
topics.description: Introduction to WebSockets for persistent, full-duplex communication, enabling real-time features like chat or live updates.

topics.name: Capstone Project Planning
topics.description: Apply all learned concepts by outlining and planning a full-stack web application project, considering architecture, features, and technologies.

weeks.title: Introduction to Databases & Relational Model Fundamentals
weeks.goal: Understand the core concepts of database systems, the relational model, and basic SQL commands for defining data structures.

topics.name: Introduction to DBMS
topics.description: Definition, purpose, advantages, disadvantages, and different types of database systems (hierarchical, network, relational, object-oriented).

topics.name: Data Models & Schemas
topics.description: Understanding conceptual, logical, and physical data models. Introduction to database schemas, instances, and data independence.

topics.name: Relational Model Concepts
topics.description: Deep dive into relations (tables), tuples (rows), attributes (columns), domains, keys (primary, foreign, candidate, super, unique).

topics.name: Relational Algebra Basics
topics.description: Fundamental operations: select, project, union, intersection, difference, Cartesian product, and join operations.

topics.name: SQL DDL: CREATE TABLE
topics.description: Introduction to SQL, Data Definition Language (DDL) commands, focusing on creating tables, defining data types, and constraints.

topics.name: ER Modeling Introduction
topics.description: Basic concepts of Entity-Relationship (ER) modeling: entities, attributes, relationships, cardinality, and participation.

weeks.title: Advanced SQL & Database Design Principles
weeks.goal: Master complex SQL queries for data manipulation and retrieval, and apply database design principles like normalization and ER modeling.

topics.name: SQL DML: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
topics.description: Data Manipulation Language (DML) commands for inserting new records, updating existing data, and deleting records from tables.

topics.name: SQL DQL: SELECT (Advanced)
topics.description: Advanced SELECT statements including WHERE clauses, ORDER BY, GROUP BY, HAVING, and aggregate functions.

topics.name: SQL Joins & Subqueries
topics.description: Understanding different types of joins (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER) and writing complex queries using subqueries.

topics.name: Views, Stored Procedures & Functions
topics.description: Introduction to creating and using views, stored procedures, and user-defined functions for database abstraction and efficiency.

topics.name: Normalization Forms (1NF, 2NF, 3NF)
topics.description: Understanding the purpose of normalization to reduce data redundancy and improve data integrity. Detailed study of 1st, 2nd, and 3rd Normal Forms.

topics.name: Normalization Forms (BCNF & Beyond)
topics.description: Exploring Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) and an introduction to 4NF and 5NF for advanced database design.

topics.name: ER to Relational Mapping
topics.description: Techniques for converting an ER model into a relational database schema, including handling various relationship types.

weeks.title: Transaction Management, Concurrency & Recovery
weeks.goal: Grasp the concepts of database transactions, ensure data consistency and integrity through concurrency control, and understand recovery mechanisms.

topics.name: Transaction Concepts & ACID Properties
topics.description: Definition of a database transaction, its states, and the importance of Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability (ACID) properties.

topics.name: Concurrency Control Issues
topics.description: Understanding problems that arise from concurrent transactions: lost update, dirty read, unrepeatable read, and phantom read.

topics.name: Locking Protocols
topics.description: Detailed study of two-phase locking (2PL) protocol, its variations, and how it ensures serializability and avoids concurrency issues.

topics.name: Timestamp-based Concurrency Control
topics.description: Understanding how timestamp ordering protocols manage concurrency without explicit locking, using read/write timestamps.

topics.name: Database Recovery Mechanisms
topics.description: Techniques for recovering from system failures, including log-based recovery (undo/redo), checkpoints, and shadow paging.

topics.name: Database Security & Authorization
topics.description: Concepts of database security, access control, user authentication, authorization (GRANT/REVOKE), and different security measures.

topics.name: Integrity Constraints & Triggers
topics.description: Review of domain, entity, and referential integrity. Introduction to triggers for enforcing complex business rules.

weeks.title: Advanced Database Concepts & Emerging Trends
weeks.goal: Explore advanced database implementation techniques, distributed systems, and contemporary database paradigms like NoSQL and Big Data.

topics.name: Indexing & Data Structures
topics.description: Understanding the importance of indexing for performance. Study of B-trees and B+ trees as primary indexing structures.

topics.name: Query Processing & Optimization
topics.description: Steps involved in query processing, query parsing, optimization techniques, and understanding query execution plans.

topics.name: Distributed Databases
topics.description: Concepts of distributed database systems, their advantages, disadvantages, data fragmentation, replication, and transparency.

topics.name: NoSQL Databases
topics.description: Introduction to NoSQL databases, their different types (Key-Value, Document, Column-Family, Graph), use cases, and the CAP theorem.

topics.name: Data Warehousing & OLAP
topics.description: Concepts of data warehousing, ETL processes, Online Analytical Processing (OLAP) vs. OLTP, and multidimensional data models.

topics.name: Big Data & Cloud Databases
topics.description: Overview of Big Data challenges and solutions. Introduction to cloud-based database services and their benefits.

topics.name: Database Administration & Tuning
topics.description: Introduction to DBA roles, backup and recovery strategies, performance monitoring, and basic database tuning techniques.

weeks.title: Introduction to Operating Systems & Process Fundamentals
weeks.goal: Understand the fundamental concepts of operating systems, their structure, and the basics of process management.

topics.name: What is an Operating System?
topics.description: Definition, role, history, and key components of an operating system.

topics.name: Operating System Structure & Services
topics.description: Explore different OS structures (monolithic, layered, microkernel) and the services an OS provides to users and programs.

topics.name: System Calls
topics.description: Understanding the interface between user programs and the OS, including types of system calls and their implementation.

topics.name: Processes: Concept & Management
topics.description: Definition of a process, process states, Process Control Blocks (PCB), and operations on processes.

topics.name: Inter-Process Communication (IPC)
topics.description: Introduction to mechanisms that allow processes to communicate and synchronize their actions, such as shared memory and message passing.

topics.name: Threads: Concept & Types
topics.description: Understanding threads as lightweight processes, user-level vs. kernel-level threads, and their benefits.

weeks.title: CPU Scheduling & Process Synchronization
weeks.goal: Learn how the CPU is efficiently allocated among competing processes and how to manage concurrent access to shared resources.

topics.name: CPU Scheduling Concepts
topics.description: Goals of CPU scheduling, types of schedulers, and evaluation criteria for scheduling algorithms.

topics.name: Common Scheduling Algorithms (Part 1)
topics.description: Detailed study of First-Come, First-Served (FCFS), Shortest-Job-First (SJF), and Priority scheduling.

topics.name: Common Scheduling Algorithms (Part 2)
topics.description: Detailed study of Round Robin, Multilevel Queue, and Multilevel Feedback Queue scheduling.

topics.name: Process Synchronization Background
topics.description: Understanding the need for synchronization, critical section problem, and race conditions.

topics.name: Mutex Locks & Semaphores
topics.description: Exploring software and hardware solutions for the critical section problem, including mutex locks and semaphores.

topics.name: Classic Synchronization Problems
topics.description: Analyzing classic problems like the Producer-Consumer, Readers-Writers, and Dining Philosophers problems and their solutions.

weeks.title: Deadlocks & Memory Management Techniques
weeks.goal: Identify conditions for deadlocks and strategies for handling them, and understand various memory management schemes.

topics.name: Deadlock Characterization
topics.description: Understanding the four necessary conditions for a deadlock to occur.

topics.name: Deadlock Prevention
topics.description: Strategies to prevent deadlocks by negating one or more of the necessary conditions.

topics.name: Deadlock Avoidance (Banker's Algorithm)
topics.description: Techniques to avoid deadlocks by dynamically checking the resource-allocation state, focusing on Banker's Algorithm.

topics.name: Deadlock Detection & Recovery
topics.description: Methods for detecting deadlocks when they occur and strategies for recovering from them.

topics.name: Memory Management Background
topics.description: Logical vs. physical address space, memory-management unit (MMU), and basic memory allocation concepts.

topics.name: Contiguous Memory Allocation
topics.description: Techniques like first-fit, best-fit, and worst-fit, along with problems like external and internal fragmentation.

topics.name: Paging & Segmentation
topics.description: Detailed study of paging (concept, hardware support, TLB) and segmentation as non-contiguous memory allocation schemes.

weeks.title: Virtual Memory, File Systems & I/O Systems
weeks.goal: Master advanced memory management techniques, understand file system design, and learn about I/O device management.

topics.name: Virtual Memory Concepts
topics.description: Introduction to virtual memory, its benefits, and the concept of demand paging.

topics.name: Page Replacement Algorithms
topics.description: Study of common page replacement algorithms: FIFO, Optimal, and LRU (Least Recently Used).

topics.name: Thrashing and Locality
topics.description: Understanding the phenomenon of thrashing and the principle of locality in memory access.

topics.name: File Concept & Access Methods
topics.description: Definition of a file, file attributes, operations, and various access methods (sequential, direct).

topics.name: Directory Structure & File Allocation Methods
topics.description: Different directory structures (single-level, two-level, tree-structured) and methods for allocating disk space (contiguous, linked, indexed).

topics.name: I/O Hardware & Application I/O Interface
topics.description: Understanding I/O devices, controllers, and the application programming interface for I/O operations.

topics.name: Kernel I/O Subsystem
topics.description: Functions of the kernel's I/O subsystem, including error handling, buffering, caching, and spooling.

weeks.title: Foundations of Cryptography
weeks.goal: Understand fundamental security concepts, classical encryption techniques, and the basics of symmetric-key cryptography.

topics.name: Introduction to Security Concepts
topics.description: Overview of information security goals (CIA triad), security attacks, services, and mechanisms.

topics.name: Classical Ciphers
topics.description: Study of historic encryption methods like Caesar cipher, Playfair, Vigenère, and their cryptanalysis.

topics.name: Symmetric Key Cryptography Principles
topics.description: Introduction to the concept of symmetric encryption, stream ciphers vs. block ciphers.

topics.name: Data Encryption Standard (DES)
topics.description: Detailed look at the DES algorithm, its structure, and its security limitations.

topics.name: Advanced Encryption Standard (AES)
topics.description: Introduction to AES, its round functions, and its role as the current standard.

topics.name: Modes of Operation for Block Ciphers
topics.description: Understanding ECB, CBC, CFB, OFB, and CTR modes and their practical implications.

weeks.title: Asymmetric Cryptography and Hashing
weeks.goal: Grasp the principles of public-key cryptography, cryptographic hash functions, and digital signatures.

topics.name: Number Theory for Cryptography
topics.description: Review of essential number theory concepts: modular arithmetic, prime numbers, Euler's totient function.

topics.name: Public Key Cryptography Principles
topics.description: Introduction to asymmetric encryption, key pairs, and the concept of public/private keys.

topics.name: RSA Algorithm
topics.description: In-depth study of the RSA encryption and decryption process, including key generation.

topics.name: Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange
topics.description: Understanding how two parties can establish a shared secret over an insecure channel.

topics.name: Cryptographic Hash Functions
topics.description: Properties of hash functions, examples (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256), and their applications.

topics.name: Message Authentication Codes (MACs)
topics.description: Concept of MACs for data integrity and authentication, comparing with hash functions.

topics.name: Digital Signatures
topics.description: How digital signatures provide authenticity, integrity, and non-repudiation, including DSA and RSA signatures.

weeks.title: Network Security Protocols and Applications
weeks.goal: Explore key management, authentication systems, and widely used security protocols for network communication.

topics.name: Key Management and Distribution
topics.description: Methods for secure key exchange, generation, storage, and revocation in cryptographic systems.

topics.name: Authentication Applications (Kerberos, X.509)
topics.description: Study of Kerberos for network authentication and X.509 certificates for public key infrastructure.

topics.name: Email Security (PGP, S/MIME)
topics.description: Protocols and standards for securing email communications, including Pretty Good Privacy and S/MIME.

topics.name: IP Security (IPSec)
topics.description: Detailed examination of IPSec architecture, authentication header (AH), and encapsulating security payload (ESP).

topics.name: Web Security (SSL/TLS)
topics.description: Understanding the Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) and Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocols for securing web traffic.

topics.name: Firewalls and Intrusion Detection Systems
topics.description: Concepts, types, and deployment of firewalls and IDSs/IPSs for network perimeter defense.

weeks.title: Advanced Security Topics and Threats
weeks.goal: Investigate common network vulnerabilities, malicious software, and emerging security challenges.

topics.name: Wireless Network Security (WEP, WPA/2/3)
topics.description: Evolution of wireless security standards, their vulnerabilities, and current best practices.

topics.name: Malicious Software (Malware)
topics.description: Categorization and analysis of viruses, worms, Trojans, ransomware, spyware, and rootkits.

topics.name: Denial of Service (DoS) and Distributed DoS (DDoS) Attacks
topics.description: Understanding DoS/DDoS attack mechanisms, impact, and mitigation strategies.

topics.name: Buffer Overflows and Exploits
topics.description: Study of common software vulnerabilities like buffer overflows, format string bugs, and their exploitation.

topics.name: Cloud Security Basics
topics.description: Introduction to security challenges and considerations in cloud computing environments (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS).

topics.name: Blockchain and Cryptocurrency Security Overview
topics.description: Basic security principles of blockchain technology and its application in cryptocurrencies.

topics.name: Ethical Hacking and Penetration Testing (Intro)
topics.description: Overview of ethical hacking methodologies, reconnaissance, scanning, and vulnerability analysis.

weeks.title: Introduction to Networks and the Physical Layer
weeks.goal: Understand fundamental network concepts, different network types, the layered architecture, and the basics of data transmission at the physical layer.

topics.name: Introduction to Computer Networks
topics.description: Definition, history, applications, and basic components of computer networks.

topics.name: Network Types and Topologies
topics.description: Explore LAN, WAN, MAN, PAN, and common network topologies (bus, star, ring, mesh, tree).

topics.name: OSI and TCP/IP Reference Models
topics.description: Detailed study of the 7-layer OSI model and the 4/5-layer TCP/IP model, their functions and comparisons.

topics.name: Physical Layer Concepts
topics.description: Understanding signals (analog vs. digital), bandwidth, throughput, and latency.

topics.name: Transmission Media
topics.description: Overview of guided (twisted pair, coaxial, fiber optic) and unguided (radio, microwave, infrared) media.

topics.name: Data Encoding and Modulation
topics.description: Techniques for converting digital data to digital signals (e.g., NRZ, Manchester) and digital data to analog signals (ASK, FSK, PSK, QAM).

weeks.title: Data Link Layer and Local Area Networks
weeks.goal: Master the principles of reliable data transfer over a link, understand error control, flow control, multiple access protocols, and the architecture of Ethernet and Wi-Fi networks.

topics.name: Data Link Layer Functions
topics.description: Framing, error control (detection and correction), flow control, and MAC addressing.

topics.name: Error Detection and Correction
topics.description: Techniques like Parity Check, Checksum, and Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC).

topics.name: Multiple Access Protocols
topics.description: Random access (ALOHA, CSMA/CD, CSMA/CA), controlled access (polling, token passing), and channelization.

topics.name: Ethernet (IEEE 802.3)
topics.description: Evolution of Ethernet, frame format, and operation of CSMA/CD.

topics.name: Switches and VLANs
topics.description: Functionality of network switches, learning MAC addresses, and configuring Virtual Local Area Networks (VLANs).

topics.name: Wireless LANs (IEEE 802.11 Wi-Fi)
topics.description: Architecture, frame format, CSMA/CA, and security aspects of Wi-Fi networks.

topics.name: ARP Protocol
topics.description: Address Resolution Protocol: Mapping IP addresses to MAC addresses.

weeks.title: Network Layer and Routing
weeks.goal: Grasp the concepts of inter-network communication, IP addressing, subnetting, and various routing protocols that enable data packets to traverse complex networks.

topics.name: Network Layer Services and Protocols
topics.description: Packetizing, logical addressing, routing, and connectionless service.

topics.name: IPv4 Addressing and Classes
topics.description: Understanding IP address structure, classes (A, B, C, D, E), and special addresses.

topics.name: Subnetting and CIDR
topics.description: Techniques for dividing networks into subnets and Classless Inter-Domain Routing for efficient IP address allocation.

topics.name: Routing Algorithms
topics.description: Study of Link State (Dijkstra's algorithm) and Distance Vector (Bellman-Ford algorithm) routing algorithms.

topics.name: Intra-domain Routing Protocols (RIP, OSPF)
topics.description: Detailed examination of Routing Information Protocol (RIP) and Open Shortest Path First (OSPF).

topics.name: Inter-domain Routing (BGP)
topics.description: Understanding the Border Gateway Protocol, path attributes, and policy-based routing.

topics.name: IPv6 and ICMP
topics.description: Introduction to IPv6 addressing, header format, and the Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) for error reporting.

weeks.title: Transport Layer, Application Layer, and Network Security
weeks.goal: Learn about end-to-end data transfer, common application protocols, and fundamental concepts of network security, including cryptography and common security mechanisms.

topics.name: Transport Layer Services
topics.description: Process-to-process communication, multiplexing/demultiplexing, and connection-oriented vs. connectionless services.

topics.name: User Datagram Protocol (UDP)
topics.description: Characteristics of UDP, header format, and its use cases for simple, fast communication.

topics.name: Transmission Control Protocol (TCP)
topics.description: Reliable data transfer, connection establishment (3-way handshake), flow control, congestion control, and TCP header.

topics.name: Application Layer Protocols (Web & Email)
topics.description: HTTP (Web), FTP (File Transfer), and email protocols: SMTP, POP3, IMAP.

topics.name: Application Layer Protocols (DNS & DHCP)
topics.description: Domain Name System (DNS) for name resolution and Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) for IP address assignment.

topics.name: Network Security Fundamentals
topics.description: Confidentiality, integrity, availability, authentication, non-repudiation, and common network threats.

topics.name: Cryptography, Firewalls, and VPNs
topics.description: Basics of symmetric and asymmetric encryption, digital signatures, firewall types, and Virtual Private Networks (VPNs).

weeks.title: Foundations of Algorithm Analysis and Basic Sorting
weeks.goal: Understand fundamental concepts of algorithm analysis, asymptotic notations, and master basic sorting algorithms and recursive techniques.

topics.name: Introduction to Algorithms
topics.description: Definition of an algorithm, characteristics, and importance in computer science.

topics.name: Asymptotic Notation (Big O, Omega, Theta)
topics.description: Understanding Big O, Big Omega, and Big Theta notations for analyzing algorithm efficiency.

topics.name: Time and Space Complexity Analysis
topics.description: Methods to analyze the worst-case, average-case, and best-case time and space complexity of algorithms.

topics.name: Recursion and Recurrence Relations
topics.description: Introduction to recursive algorithms, writing recurrence relations, and solving them using master theorem or substitution method.

topics.name: Elementary Sorting Algorithms
topics.description: Analysis and implementation of Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Insertion Sort.

topics.name: Review of Essential Data Structures
topics.description: Brief review of arrays, linked lists, stacks, and queues and their algorithmic implications.

weeks.title: Advanced Sorting, Divide & Conquer, and Hashing
weeks.goal: Explore efficient sorting techniques, master the divide and conquer paradigm, and understand hashing for efficient data retrieval.

topics.name: Divide and Conquer Paradigm
topics.description: Understanding the general structure and application of divide and conquer algorithms.

topics.name: Merge Sort
topics.description: Detailed analysis and implementation of Merge Sort, a classic divide and conquer sorting algorithm.

topics.name: Quick Sort
topics.description: Detailed analysis and implementation of Quick Sort, including pivot selection strategies and its average-case performance.

topics.name: Heap Sort and Heaps
topics.description: Understanding heap data structure (max-heap, min-heap) and its application in Heap Sort.

topics.name: Hashing and Hash Tables
topics.description: Concepts of hashing, hash functions, collision resolution techniques (chaining, open addressing).

topics.name: Introduction to Greedy Algorithms
topics.description: Understanding the greedy approach with simple examples like Activity Selection Problem or Change-making problem.

topics.name: Counting, Radix, and Bucket Sort
topics.description: Understanding non-comparison-based sorting algorithms and their specific use cases.

weeks.title: Graph Algorithms and Dynamic Programming Fundamentals
weeks.goal: Master graph representation and traversal algorithms, understand shortest path and minimum spanning tree problems, and begin dynamic programming.

topics.name: Graph Representations
topics.description: Adjacency matrix and adjacency list representations of graphs and their trade-offs.

topics.name: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
topics.description: Algorithm, implementation, and applications of BFS for graph traversal.

topics.name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
topics.description: Algorithm, implementation, and applications of DFS, including topological sort and strongly connected components.

topics.name: Minimum Spanning Trees (Prim's and Kruskal's)
topics.description: Understanding MST problem and implementing Prim's and Kruskal's algorithms.

topics.name: Shortest Path Algorithms (Dijkstra's)
topics.description: Dijkstra's algorithm for single-source shortest paths on graphs with non-negative edge weights.

topics.name: Introduction to Dynamic Programming
topics.description: Concepts of optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems, memoization vs. tabulation.

topics.name: DP: Fibonacci and 0/1 Knapsack (Intro)
topics.description: Solving classic DP problems like Fibonacci sequence and an introduction to the 0/1 Knapsack problem.

weeks.title: Advanced Dynamic Programming, Flow Networks, and Complexity Theory
weeks.goal: Deepen understanding of dynamic programming, explore network flow, and grasp the basics of computational complexity theory including P, NP, and NP-completeness.

topics.name: Dynamic Programming: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)
topics.description: Detailed analysis and implementation of the LCS problem using dynamic programming.

topics.name: Dynamic Programming: Matrix Chain Multiplication
topics.description: Solving the matrix chain multiplication problem using dynamic programming to find optimal parenthesization.

topics.name: All-Pairs Shortest Path (Floyd-Warshall)
topics.description: Understanding and implementing the Floyd-Warshall algorithm for finding shortest paths between all pairs of vertices.

topics.name: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
topics.description: Shortest path algorithm for graphs with negative edge weights, including detection of negative cycles.

topics.name: Introduction to Network Flow
topics.description: Concepts of flow networks, capacities, sources, sinks, and the Max-Flow Min-Cut theorem.

topics.name: P, NP, and NP-Completeness
topics.description: Understanding complexity classes P and NP, NP-hard and NP-complete problems, and polynomial-time reductions.

topics.name: Approximation and Randomized Algorithms (Intro)
topics.description: Brief overview of approximation algorithms for NP-hard problems and randomized algorithms (e.g., Karger's Min-Cut).

weeks.title: Foundations of Probability
weeks.goal: Understand basic probability concepts, set theory, counting techniques, and conditional probability.

topics.name: Introduction to Probability
topics.description: Definition of probability, experiments, outcomes, sample space, and events.

topics.name: Set Theory and Events
topics.description: Review of set operations (union, intersection, complement) applied to events, Venn diagrams.

topics.name: Basic Probability Rules
topics.description: Addition rule for mutually exclusive events, general addition rule, multiplication rule.

topics.name: Conditional Probability
topics.description: Definition of conditional probability, calculating probabilities given an event has occurred.

topics.name: Independence of Events
topics.description: Understanding and testing for independent events, implications for probability calculations.

topics.name: Bayes' Theorem
topics.description: Applying Bayes' theorem to update probabilities based on new evidence.

topics.name: Counting Techniques
topics.description: Permutations and combinations, and their application in probability problems.

weeks.title: Random Variables and Probability Distributions
weeks.goal: Grasp the concept of random variables and become familiar with common discrete and continuous probability distributions.

topics.name: Random Variables (Discrete & Continuous)
topics.description: Definition and types of random variables, distinguishing between discrete and continuous.

topics.name: PMF, PDF, and CDF
topics.description: Understanding Probability Mass Functions (PMF), Probability Density Functions (PDF), and Cumulative Distribution Functions (CDF).

topics.name: Expected Value and Variance
topics.description: Calculating the mean (expected value) and variance of discrete and continuous random variables.

topics.name: Common Discrete Distributions
topics.description: Properties and applications of Bernoulli, Binomial, and Poisson distributions.

topics.name: Continuous Distributions (Uniform, Exponential)
topics.description: Understanding the Uniform and Exponential distributions and their use cases.

topics.name: The Normal Distribution
topics.description: Properties of the normal distribution, standard normal distribution, and Z-scores.

topics.name: Central Limit Theorem (Introduction)
topics.description: Introduction to the Central Limit Theorem and its significance for sampling distributions.

weeks.title: Descriptive Statistics and Sampling Distributions
weeks.goal: Learn how to summarize, visualize, and analyze data, and understand the principles of sampling and sampling distributions.

topics.name: Types of Data and Measurement Scales
topics.description: Categorical vs. numerical data, nominal, ordinal, interval, and ratio scales.

topics.name: Measures of Central Tendency
topics.description: Calculating and interpreting mean, median, and mode for various datasets.

topics.name: Measures of Dispersion
topics.description: Understanding range, variance, standard deviation, and interquartile range (IQR).

topics.name: Data Visualization
topics.description: Creating and interpreting histograms, box plots, bar charts, and scatter plots.

topics.name: Sampling Methods
topics.description: Introduction to different sampling techniques: simple random, stratified, cluster, and systematic sampling.

topics.name: Sampling Distributions
topics.description: Understanding the concept of sampling distributions, particularly for the sample mean and proportion.

topics.name: Point Estimation and Estimators
topics.description: Introduction to point estimates and properties of good estimators (unbiasedness, efficiency).

weeks.title: Inferential Statistics: Estimation and Hypothesis Testing
weeks.goal: Apply statistical inference techniques to estimate population parameters and test hypotheses using sample data.

topics.name: Introduction to Statistical Inference
topics.description: Overview of inferential statistics, distinguishing from descriptive statistics.

topics.name: Confidence Intervals for the Mean
topics.description: Constructing and interpreting confidence intervals for population means (Z and T-intervals).

topics.name: Confidence Intervals for Proportions
topics.description: Constructing and interpreting confidence intervals for population proportions.

topics.name: Introduction to Hypothesis Testing
topics.description: Formulating null and alternative hypotheses, Type I and Type II errors, significance level.

topics.name: Z-tests for Means and Proportions
topics.description: Performing one-sample Z-tests for population means and proportions.

topics.name: T-tests (One-sample, Two-sample)
topics.description: Performing one-sample, independent two-sample, and paired T-tests for means.

topics.name: Introduction to Simple Linear Regression
topics.description: Basic concepts of correlation, linear regression model, and interpreting regression coefficients.

weeks.title: Week 1: Python Fundamentals and Control Flow
weeks.goal: Understand the basic syntax, data types, and fundamental control structures of Python programming.

topics.name: Introduction to Python & Environment Setup
topics.description: Learn what Python is, its applications, and how to install Python and set up a development environment (e.g., VS Code, Jupyter).

topics.name: Variables and Basic Data Types
topics.description: Explore Python's fundamental data types (integers, floats, strings, booleans) and how to declare and use variables.

topics.name: Operators and Expressions
topics.description: Study arithmetic, comparison, logical, assignment, and identity operators, and how to form expressions.

topics.name: Basic Input and Output
topics.description: Learn how to take user input using `input()` and display output using `print()` with various formatting options.

topics.name: Conditional Statements (if, elif, else)
topics.description: Understand how to control program flow based on conditions using `if`, `elif`, and `else` statements.

topics.name: Loops (for and while)
topics.description: Master iteration using `for` loops (with `range()`, iterables) and `while` loops, including `break` and `continue`.

topics.name: Defining and Calling Functions
topics.description: Learn to define reusable blocks of code with `def`, pass arguments, and return values. Introduce docstrings.

weeks.title: Week 2: Data Structures and Advanced Functions
weeks.goal: Gain proficiency in Python's built-in data structures and explore more advanced function concepts, including error handling.

topics.name: Lists: Creation and Manipulation
topics.description: Deep dive into lists – creating, accessing, slicing, adding, removing, and modifying elements. Introduction to list comprehensions.

topics.name: Tuples: Immutability and Use Cases
topics.description: Understand tuples as immutable sequences, their creation, packing/unpacking, and scenarios where they are preferred over lists.

topics.name: Dictionaries: Key-Value Pairs
topics.description: Explore dictionaries for mapping key-value pairs – creation, access, modification, and common dictionary methods.

topics.name: Sets: Unique Elements and Operations
topics.description: Learn about sets for storing unique elements and performing set operations like union, intersection, and difference.

topics.name: Advanced String Manipulation and Formatting
topics.description: Master string methods (e.g., `split`, `join`, `find`, `replace`) and various formatting techniques (f-strings, `format()`).

topics.name: Function Scope and Advanced Arguments
topics.description: Understand local vs. global scope, and advanced function arguments (`*args`, `**kwargs`, default values, keyword arguments).

topics.name: Error Handling (try-except-finally)
topics.description: Learn to gracefully handle runtime errors and exceptions using `try`, `except`, `else`, and `finally` blocks.

weeks.title: Week 3: Object-Oriented Programming and Modules
weeks.goal: Grasp the core principles of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) in Python and learn to organize code using modules and handle files.

topics.name: Introduction to Classes and Objects (OOP)
topics.description: Understand the fundamental concepts of Object-Oriented Programming, including classes as blueprints and objects as instances.

topics.name: Attributes and Methods
topics.description: Learn to define instance attributes, class attributes, and instance methods within classes. Understand `self` parameter.

topics.name: Inheritance: Base and Derived Classes
topics.description: Explore how to create new classes (derived/child) from existing ones (base/parent) to promote code reuse.

topics.name: Polymorphism and Method Overriding
topics.description: Understand polymorphism as the ability of objects of different classes to respond to the same method call. Learn method overriding.

topics.name: Encapsulation and Properties
topics.description: Learn about data hiding and bundling data with methods. Explore using properties (`@property` decorator) for controlled attribute access.

topics.name: Modules and Packages
topics.description: Understand how to organize code into modules and packages, and how to import and use them effectively.

topics.name: File I/O Operations
topics.description: Learn to read from and write to files using various modes (read, write, append) and the `with` statement for automatic resource management.

weeks.title: Week 4: Advanced Concepts, Best Practices, and Libraries
weeks.goal: Delve into more advanced Python features, understand best practices for writing clean code, and get an introduction to using external libraries.

topics.name: Generators and Iterators
topics.description: Explore generators (using `yield`) and iterators for memory-efficient processing of large datasets.

topics.name: Decorators
topics.description: Understand decorators as a way to modify or enhance functions or methods without changing their source code.

topics.name: Context Managers (with statement)
topics.description: Learn to create and use custom context managers using classes or the `@contextlib.contextmanager` decorator.

topics.name: Virtual Environments
topics.description: Understand the importance of virtual environments (`venv`) for managing project dependencies and avoiding conflicts.

topics.name: Introduction to Standard and External Libraries
topics.description: Overview of key modules in the Python Standard Library (e.g., `os`, `sys`, `math`, `datetime`) and the concept of `pip` for external packages.

topics.name: Debugging Techniques
topics.description: Learn effective strategies for debugging Python code, including using print statements, logging, and basic debugger usage (e.g., `pdb`).

topics.name: Best Practices, PEP 8, and Code Style
topics.description: Understand Pythonic coding conventions, the PEP 8 style guide, and practices for writing readable, maintainable, and efficient code.

weeks.title: Week 1: Java Fundamentals and Basic Syntax
weeks.goal: Understand the core concepts of Java, set up the development environment, and master basic programming constructs like variables, operators, control flow, and arrays.

topics.name: Introduction to Java, JVM, JDK, JRE
topics.description: Learn what Java is, its history, and the roles of Java Virtual Machine (JVM), Java Development Kit (JDK), and Java Runtime Environment (JRE). Set up your development environment.

topics.name: Basic Syntax, Variables, and Data Types
topics.description: Explore Java's fundamental syntax, how to declare variables, and understand primitive data types (int, double, boolean, char, etc.) and string basics.

topics.name: Operators and Expressions
topics.description: Cover arithmetic, relational, logical, assignment, and unary operators. Understand operator precedence and how to build expressions.

topics.name: Control Flow Statements (if-else, switch)
topics.description: Learn how to control the flow of execution in a program using conditional statements like 'if-else', 'else-if', and 'switch'.

topics.name: Looping Constructs (for, while, do-while)
topics.description: Master iterative statements: 'for' loops (including enhanced for loop), 'while' loops, and 'do-while' loops for repetitive tasks.

topics.name: Arrays and Introduction to Methods
topics.description: Understand how to declare, initialize, and manipulate one-dimensional arrays. Get an introduction to defining and calling methods.

weeks.title: Week 2: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) I
weeks.goal: Grasp the fundamental principles of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) in Java, including classes, objects, encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism.

topics.name: Classes and Objects
topics.description: Dive into the core of OOP: defining classes as blueprints and creating objects (instances) from them. Understand instance variables and methods.

topics.name: Constructors
topics.description: Learn about constructors, special methods used for initializing objects. Explore default, parameterized, and copy constructors.

topics.name: Encapsulation (Getters and Setters)
topics.description: Understand encapsulation as a principle of bundling data and methods that operate on the data within a single unit. Implement 'getter' and 'setter' methods.

topics.name: Inheritance (is-a relationship)
topics.description: Explore inheritance, a mechanism where one class acquires the properties and behaviors of another class. Understand 'extends' keyword and method overriding.

topics.name: Polymorphism (Overloading and Overriding)
topics.description: Learn about polymorphism, the ability of an object to take on many forms. Focus on method overloading (compile-time) and method overriding (runtime).

topics.name: Abstract Classes and Interfaces
topics.description: Differentiate between abstract classes and interfaces, understanding their roles in achieving abstraction and defining contracts in Java.

weeks.title: Week 3: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) II & Collections
weeks.goal: Deepen understanding of advanced OOP concepts like exception handling and packages, and gain proficiency in using the Java Collections Framework for efficient data management.

topics.name: Exception Handling (try-catch-finally)
topics.description: Learn how to manage runtime errors using 'try', 'catch', 'finally' blocks. Understand checked vs. unchecked exceptions and throwing custom exceptions.

topics.name: Packages and Access Modifiers
topics.description: Understand how to organize classes into packages and control visibility using access modifiers (public, private, protected, default).

topics.name: Introduction to Java Collections Framework
topics.description: Get an overview of the Collections Framework, its hierarchy, and the benefits of using pre-built data structures.

topics.name: List Interface and Implementations (ArrayList, LinkedList)
topics.description: Explore the 'List' interface and its common implementations: 'ArrayList' (dynamic array) and 'LinkedList' (doubly linked list), understanding their use cases.

topics.name: Set Interface and Implementations (HashSet, TreeSet)
topics.description: Learn about the 'Set' interface for unique elements. Study 'HashSet' (unordered, fast) and 'TreeSet' (ordered, sorted).

topics.name: Map Interface and Implementations (HashMap, TreeMap)
topics.description: Understand the 'Map' interface for key-value pairs. Focus on 'HashMap' (unordered, fast) and 'TreeMap' (ordered by key).

weeks.title: Week 4: Modern Java Features & File Handling
weeks.goal: Explore modern Java features like Generics, Lambda Expressions, and the Stream API, alongside essential topics like File I/O and an introduction to multithreading.

topics.name: Generics
topics.description: Learn about Generics for creating type-safe and reusable code, reducing runtime errors. Understand generic classes, interfaces, and methods.

topics.name: Lambda Expressions
topics.description: Introduce Lambda Expressions as a concise way to represent an anonymous function, especially useful for functional interfaces.

topics.name: Stream API (Basics: filter, map, forEach)
topics.description: Get an introduction to the Stream API for processing collections of objects. Learn basic operations like 'filter', 'map', and 'forEach'.

topics.name: File I/O Basics (FileReader, FileWriter, BufferedReader)
topics.description: Understand how to perform basic file input and output operations in Java, including reading from and writing to text files using character streams.

topics.name: Serialization and Deserialization
topics.description: Learn about object serialization, the process of converting an object into a byte stream, and deserialization, reconstructing the object from the stream.

topics.name: Introduction to Multithreading (Threads, Runnable)
topics.description: Explore the basics of concurrent programming using threads. Understand how to create and run threads using the 'Thread' class and 'Runnable' interface.

weeks.title: Foundations of Life: Chemistry, Cells, and Energy
weeks.goal: Understand the fundamental chemical principles, cellular structures, and basic energy processes that define life.

topics.name: Introduction to Biology & Scientific Method
topics.description: Overview of biology as a science, its major themes, and the process of scientific inquiry.

topics.name: Chemistry of Life: Atoms, Molecules, and Water
topics.description: Review of basic chemistry relevant to biology, including atomic structure, chemical bonds, and the unique properties of water.

topics.name: Organic Macromolecules (Carbohydrates, Lipids, Proteins, Nucleic Acids)
topics.description: Structure and function of the four major classes of organic molecules essential for life.

topics.name: Cell Structure: Prokaryotes vs. Eukaryotes
topics.description: Comparative study of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell organization, highlighting key differences and similarities.

topics.name: Eukaryotic Cell Organelles and Their Functions
topics.description: Detailed examination of the structure and specific roles of major organelles within eukaryotic cells (e.g., nucleus, mitochondria, ER, Golgi).

topics.name: Membrane Structure and Transport
topics.description: Understanding the fluid mosaic model of cell membranes and the various mechanisms of substance transport across them (passive, active, bulk).

topics.name: Introduction to Metabolism and Enzymes
topics.description: Basic concepts of cellular metabolism, energy flow, and the role of enzymes as biological catalysts.

weeks.title: Genetics: Inheritance, DNA, and Gene Expression
weeks.goal: Comprehend the mechanisms of heredity, the structure and replication of DNA, and the processes of gene expression and regulation.

topics.name: The Cell Cycle and Mitosis
topics.description: Phases of the cell cycle and the process of somatic cell division (mitosis) for growth and repair.

topics.name: Meiosis and Sexual Reproduction
topics.description: Understanding the process of gamete formation (meiosis) and its role in genetic variation and sexual reproduction.

topics.name: Mendelian Genetics and Inheritance Patterns
topics.description: Principles of Mendelian inheritance, monohybrid and dihybrid crosses, and probability in genetics.

topics.name: Non-Mendelian Inheritance Patterns
topics.description: Exploring more complex inheritance patterns like incomplete dominance, codominance, multiple alleles, and polygenic inheritance.

topics.name: DNA Structure and Replication
topics.description: Detailed study of the double helix structure of DNA and the semi-conservative mechanism of DNA replication.

topics.name: Gene Expression: Transcription and Translation
topics.description: The central dogma of molecular biology: how genetic information flows from DNA to RNA (transcription) and then to protein (translation).

topics.name: Gene Regulation and Mutations
topics.description: Mechanisms by which gene expression is controlled in prokaryotes and eukaryotes, and the impact of mutations on genetic information.

weeks.title: Evolution and the Diversity of Life
weeks.goal: Explore the theory of evolution, the mechanisms that drive it, and the vast diversity of life on Earth through classification.

topics.name: Introduction to Evolution and Darwin's Theory
topics.description: Historical context of evolutionary thought, Darwin's theory of natural selection, and its core principles.

topics.name: Evidence for Evolution
topics.description: Examination of various lines of evidence supporting evolution, including fossils, comparative anatomy, embryology, and molecular biology.

topics.name: Mechanisms of Evolution (Genetic Drift, Gene Flow, Mutation)
topics.description: Understanding the forces that cause changes in allele frequencies in populations, beyond natural selection.

topics.name: Speciation and Phylogeny
topics.description: How new species arise (speciation) and the use of phylogenetic trees to represent evolutionary relationships.

topics.name: Classification of Life: Domains and Kingdoms
topics.description: Overview of the hierarchical classification system (taxonomy) and the characteristics of the three domains and major kingdoms of life.

topics.name: Prokaryotes (Bacteria, Archaea) and Viruses
topics.description: Characteristics, diversity, and ecological roles of bacteria, archaea, and the basic biology of viruses.

topics.name: Protists and Fungi
topics.description: Diversity, key features, and ecological importance of protists and fungi.

weeks.title: Ecology and Introduction to Plant/Animal Systems
weeks.goal: Grasp fundamental ecological principles, explore interactions within ecosystems, and gain an introductory understanding of plant and animal form and function.

topics.name: Ecosystems and Biomes
topics.description: Structure and function of ecosystems, energy flow, nutrient cycling, and the characteristics of major terrestrial and aquatic biomes.

topics.name: Population Ecology
topics.description: Study of population dynamics, growth models, and factors affecting population size and distribution.

topics.name: Community Ecology and Species Interactions
topics.description: Analysis of interactions between species within a community (competition, predation, symbiosis) and concepts like ecological succession.

topics.name: Plant Structure and Function (Roots, Stems, Leaves)
topics.description: Basic anatomy of vascular plants and the functions of their major organs in growth, photosynthesis, and transport.

topics.name: Plant Reproduction and Growth
topics.description: Overview of plant life cycles, reproduction (sexual and asexual), and mechanisms of plant growth and development.

topics.name: Introduction to Animal Tissues and Organ Systems
topics.description: Overview of the four main types of animal tissues and an introduction to the major organ systems (e.g., digestive, circulatory, nervous).

topics.name: Homeostasis and Regulation in Animals
topics.description: The concept of homeostasis and how animals maintain stable internal conditions through feedback mechanisms.

weeks.title: Introduction to Mechanics: Kinematics and Dynamics
weeks.goal: Establish a strong foundation in describing motion and understanding the forces that cause it, using Newton's laws.

topics.name: Kinematics in One Dimension
topics.description: Study of motion along a straight line, including displacement, velocity, and acceleration.

topics.name: Kinematics in Two Dimensions
topics.description: Analysis of projectile motion and uniform circular motion using vector components.

topics.name: Newton's Laws of Motion
topics.description: Understanding inertia, force, mass, and action-reaction pairs as fundamental principles of dynamics.

topics.name: Applications of Newton's Laws
topics.description: Solving problems involving forces, free-body diagrams, and various types of forces like tension and normal force.

topics.name: Friction and Drag Forces
topics.description: Exploring static and kinetic friction, and resistive forces in fluids.

topics.name: Work and Kinetic Energy
topics.description: Definition of work done by a force and the work-energy theorem.

weeks.title: Energy, Momentum, and Rotational Motion
weeks.goal: Master the concepts of energy and momentum conservation, and introduce the principles of rotational dynamics.

topics.name: Potential Energy and Conservation of Energy
topics.description: Understanding gravitational and elastic potential energy, and applying the principle of conservation of mechanical energy.

topics.name: Power
topics.description: Definition of power as the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred.

topics.name: Linear Momentum and Impulse
topics.description: Introduction to linear momentum and impulse, and the impulse-momentum theorem.

topics.name: Conservation of Linear Momentum and Collisions
topics.description: Applying the conservation of momentum to various types of collisions (elastic and inelastic).

topics.name: Rotational Kinematics
topics.description: Describing rotational motion using angular displacement, velocity, and acceleration.

topics.name: Torque and Angular Momentum
topics.description: Understanding torque as the rotational equivalent of force, and the concept of angular momentum.

topics.name: Conservation of Angular Momentum
topics.description: Applying the conservation of angular momentum to systems with changing moments of inertia.

weeks.title: Oscillations, Waves, and Thermodynamics
weeks.goal: Explore periodic motion, the properties of waves, and the fundamental laws governing heat and energy transfer.

topics.name: Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM)
topics.description: Analysis of oscillatory motion, including springs and pendulums, and their characteristic equations.

topics.name: Wave Properties
topics.description: Introduction to transverse and longitudinal waves, wavelength, frequency, amplitude, and wave speed.

topics.name: Sound Waves
topics.description: Understanding the nature of sound, intensity, pitch, and the Doppler effect.

topics.name: Temperature and Heat
topics.description: Definitions of temperature scales, thermal expansion, and the concept of heat as energy transfer.

topics.name: Heat Transfer Mechanisms
topics.description: Study of conduction, convection, and radiation as methods of heat transfer.

topics.name: First Law of Thermodynamics
topics.description: Relating internal energy, heat, and work; understanding energy conservation in thermodynamic systems.

topics.name: Second Law of Thermodynamics
topics.description: Concepts of entropy, heat engines, refrigerators, and the direction of natural processes.

weeks.title: Electromagnetism: Electrostatics and DC Circuits
weeks.goal: Introduce the fundamental concepts of electric charge, fields, potential, and basic direct current circuits.

topics.name: Electric Charge and Coulomb's Law
topics.description: Understanding charge quantization, conservation, and the force between point charges.

topics.name: Electric Fields
topics.description: Concept of electric field, field lines, and calculating fields due to various charge distributions.

topics.name: Electric Potential and Potential Energy
topics.description: Definition of electric potential energy and electric potential (voltage), and their relation to electric fields.

topics.name: Capacitance and Dielectrics
topics.description: Understanding capacitors, their ability to store charge, and the effect of dielectric materials.

topics.name: Electric Current and Resistance
topics.description: Definitions of current, resistance, resistivity, and Ohm's Law.

topics.name: DC Circuits: Series and Parallel
topics.description: Analyzing simple direct current circuits with resistors connected in series and parallel.

topics.name: Kirchhoff's Rules
topics.description: Applying Kirchhoff's current and voltage laws to solve more complex DC circuits.

weeks.title: Foundations: Functions, Limits, and Continuity
weeks.goal: Establish a strong understanding of functions, grasp the concept of limits, and apply continuity principles to various functions.

topics.name: Review of Functions
topics.description: Recap of function definitions, domain, range, graphs, types of functions (polynomial, rational, exponential, logarithmic, trigonometric).

topics.name: Introduction to Limits
topics.description: Intuitive understanding of limits, graphical approach, one-sided limits, and limits that do not exist.

topics.name: Properties of Limits
topics.description: Algebraic properties of limits, direct substitution, and indeterminate forms (0/0).

topics.name: Continuity
topics.description: Definition of continuity, types of discontinuities, and continuity on an interval.

topics.name: Limits at Infinity and Asymptotes
topics.description: Evaluating limits as x approaches infinity, horizontal and vertical asymptotes.

topics.name: Intermediate Value Theorem
topics.description: Understanding and applying the Intermediate Value Theorem for continuous functions.

weeks.title: Differentiation: Core Concepts and Rules
weeks.goal: Understand the definition of the derivative, master fundamental differentiation rules, and apply them to various function types.

topics.name: Introduction to Derivatives
topics.description: Geometric interpretation as tangent lines, instantaneous rates of change, and average rates of change.

topics.name: Definition of the Derivative
topics.description: Using the limit definition of the derivative to find derivatives of functions.

topics.name: Basic Differentiation Rules
topics.description: Power Rule, Constant Multiple Rule, Sum and Difference Rules.

topics.name: Product and Quotient Rules
topics.description: Applying the product and quotient rules for differentiating complex functions.

topics.name: Chain Rule
topics.description: Mastering the chain rule for composite functions.

topics.name: Derivatives of Trigonometric Functions
topics.description: Learning and applying derivatives of sine, cosine, tangent, cotangent, secant, and cosecant.

topics.name: Implicit Differentiation
topics.description: Techniques for differentiating implicitly defined functions.

weeks.title: Applications of Differentiation
weeks.goal: Apply differentiation techniques to solve real-world problems involving rates of change, optimization, and curve sketching.

topics.name: Related Rates
topics.description: Solving problems where several quantities are related and all are changing with respect to time.

topics.name: Extrema on an Interval
topics.description: Finding absolute and local extrema of functions on closed intervals, critical numbers.

topics.name: Optimization Problems
topics.description: Using derivatives to find maximum or minimum values in applied contexts.

topics.name: Mean Value Theorem
topics.description: Understanding and applying Rolle's Theorem and the Mean Value Theorem.

topics.name: First and Second Derivative Tests
topics.description: Using derivatives to determine increasing/decreasing intervals, concavity, and points of inflection.

topics.name: Curve Sketching
topics.description: Combining all derivative tests to accurately sketch graphs of functions.

topics.name: L'Hôpital's Rule
topics.description: Using L'Hôpital's Rule to evaluate indeterminate forms of limits (0/0, ∞/∞).

weeks.title: Introduction to Integration
weeks.goal: Understand antiderivatives, grasp the concept of definite integrals, and learn basic integration techniques and applications.

topics.name: Antiderivatives and Indefinite Integrals
topics.description: Introduction to antiderivatives, the power rule for integration, and properties of indefinite integrals.

topics.name: Definite Integrals and Riemann Sums
topics.description: Understanding definite integrals as net area, approximating area with Riemann sums (left, right, midpoint).

topics.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus (Part 1)
topics.description: Connecting differentiation and integration; evaluating definite integrals using antiderivatives.

topics.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus (Part 2)
topics.description: Understanding the derivative of an integral with a variable upper limit.

topics.name: U-Substitution (Integration by Substitution)
topics.description: Mastering the technique of u-substitution for integrating composite functions.

topics.name: Applications: Area Between Curves
topics.description: Calculating the area bounded by two or more curves using definite integrals.

topics.name: Applications: Average Value of a Function
topics.description: Calculating the average value of a function over a given interval.

weeks.title: Foundations: Functions, Limits, and Continuity
weeks.goal: Establish a strong understanding of functions, their properties, and the fundamental concept of limits and continuity, which are essential prerequisites for derivatives.

topics.name: Functions and Their Graphs
topics.description: Reviewing types of functions (polynomial, rational, exponential, logarithmic, trigonometric), domain, range, and graph interpretation.

topics.name: Introduction to Limits
topics.description: Understanding the intuitive definition of a limit, one-sided limits, and limits from graphs and tables.

topics.name: Calculating Limits Using Limit Laws
topics.description: Applying algebraic techniques (direct substitution, factoring, rationalizing) to evaluate limits.

topics.name: Limits at Infinity and Asymptotes
topics.description: Analyzing limits as x approaches infinity and identifying horizontal and vertical asymptotes.

topics.name: Continuity of Functions
topics.description: Defining continuity at a point and on an interval, and identifying types of discontinuities.

topics.name: The Intermediate Value Theorem (IVT)
topics.description: Understanding and applying the IVT to show the existence of roots or specific function values.

topics.name: Review and Problem Solving: Limits and Continuity
topics.description: Consolidating understanding through practice problems covering all topics from the week.

weeks.title: The Derivative: Definition, Rules, and Basic Applications
weeks.goal: Master the definition of the derivative and all fundamental differentiation rules, including the Chain Rule, to compute derivatives of various functions.

topics.name: The Definition of the Derivative
topics.description: Understanding the derivative as a limit (slope of tangent line, instantaneous rate of change) and computing derivatives from first principles.

topics.name: Basic Differentiation Rules
topics.description: Applying the Power Rule, Constant Multiple Rule, Sum/Difference Rule, and derivatives of exponential functions.

topics.name: Product and Quotient Rules
topics.description: Learning and applying rules for differentiating products and quotients of functions.

topics.name: Derivatives of Trigonometric Functions
topics.description: Memorizing and applying differentiation rules for sine, cosine, tangent, and other trigonometric functions.

topics.name: The Chain Rule
topics.description: Understanding and applying the Chain Rule for composite functions, a crucial differentiation technique.

topics.name: Higher-Order Derivatives
topics.description: Calculating second, third, and higher derivatives and understanding their interpretations.

topics.name: Derivatives of Logarithmic Functions
topics.description: Applying differentiation rules for natural logarithms and general logarithmic functions, including logarithmic differentiation.

weeks.title: Applications of Differentiation: Curve Sketching and Optimization
weeks.goal: Apply derivatives to analyze function behavior, sketch graphs, solve optimization problems, and understand related rates.

topics.name: Implicit Differentiation
topics.description: Differentiating equations where y is not explicitly defined as a function of x, and finding slopes of tangent lines to implicit curves.

topics.name: Related Rates
topics.description: Solving problems involving rates of change of two or more related quantities with respect to time.

topics.name: Extrema and Critical Points
topics.description: Finding absolute and local maximum/minimum values of functions and identifying critical numbers.

topics.name: The Mean Value Theorem (MVT) and Rolle's Theorem
topics.description: Understanding and applying the MVT to relate average and instantaneous rates of change.

topics.name: The First Derivative Test and Monotonicity
topics.description: Using the first derivative to determine intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing, and to classify local extrema.

topics.name: Concavity and Inflection Points (Second Derivative Test)
topics.description: Using the second derivative to determine concavity, find inflection points, and classify local extrema.

topics.name: Curve Sketching
topics.description: Combining all derivative tests and limit analysis to accurately sketch the graph of a function.

weeks.title: Optimization, L'Hôpital's Rule, and Differentials
weeks.goal: Solve complex optimization problems, evaluate indeterminate forms using L'Hôpital's Rule, and understand linear approximations and differentials.

topics.name: Optimization Problems
topics.description: Applying differentiation techniques to solve real-world problems involving maximizing or minimizing quantities.

topics.name: Indeterminate Forms and L'Hôpital's Rule (Part 1)
topics.description: Understanding indeterminate forms (0/0, ∞/∞) and applying L'Hôpital's Rule.

topics.name: L'Hôpital's Rule (Part 2)
topics.description: Applying L'Hôpital's Rule to other indeterminate forms (0·∞, ∞-∞, 1^∞, 0^0, ∞^0) after algebraic manipulation.

topics.name: Linear Approximations and Differentials
topics.description: Using the tangent line to approximate function values and understanding differentials (dy, dx).

topics.name: Newton's Method (Optional/Intro)
topics.description: An iterative method for finding approximate roots of a real-valued function.

topics.name: Introduction to Antiderivatives
topics.description: Understanding the inverse process of differentiation and finding general antiderivatives.

topics.name: Comprehensive Review and Practice Exam
topics.description: A thorough review of all Differential Calculus concepts and techniques, followed by a practice exam to prepare for assessments.
